theory.canonical_statement: ID 477 formalizes the operational resilience of the system at human body temperature (310 K). While the core remains cryogenic, interface logic functions via scale invariant properties of the Wess Zumino Witten topological action. This maintains state stability despite the thermal gradients between the user and the reactor. Thermal Adaptation: Adjusts coupling constants to maintain superpositions at biological temperatures. Superconducting Simulation: Uses sub cellular coherence to mimic the low entropy states of the extraction channels.
